pattern: Climbing a complete binary tree by halving the label while correcting for the alternating left-to-right and right-to-left numbering of each row.
hints: Without the zigzag, a node's parent label is just the label divided by two.
Within a row the labels are mirrored on alternate levels, so the 'normal' position and the actual label are reflections within that row's value range.

Find the level from the label, then repeatedly map the current label to its mirror within the row to get its normal index, halve to the parent's normal index, mirror that back to a real label, and collect labels until you reach the root.

edge_inputs.description: first example on an even row
second example reaching a deeper row
a node on the second (reversed) row
a node on the second row, other end
a label at a row boundary
large label deep in the tree

stress.description: random labels across the full range to exercise both parities of rows
stress.note: size ignored; single label per case

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def pathInZigZagTree(self, label):
        level = label.bit_length() - 1
        path = []
        cur = label
        while cur >= 1:
            path.append(cur)
            lo = 1 << level
            hi = (1 << (level + 1)) - 1
            mirrored = lo + hi - cur
            cur = mirrored // 2
            level -= 1
        path.reverse()
        return path
